qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in MarshallingDecoder allows remote code execution via readObject on untrusted network data
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns codec-marshalling MarshallingDecoder/CompatibleMarshallingDecoder calling Unmarshaller.readObject() on network bytes; the advisory concerns codec-http2 DefaultHttp2FrameReader accepting unlimited zero-byte CONTINUATION frames without a frame-count limit.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described Netty HTTP/2 CONTINUATION-frame flood root cause. The only qualifying final finding is an unrelated MarshallingDecoder unsafe-deserialization RCE. The HTTP/2 finding in the session is status=no_vuln and describes mitigations, including a small CONTINUATION fragment limit, rather than reporting the advisory's missing count limit and zero-byte frame bypass as a qualifying vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-w9fj-cfpg-grvv identifies codec-http2/src/main/java/io/netty/handler/codec/http2/DefaultHttp2FrameReader.java verifyContinuationFrame() and HeadersBlockBuilder.addFragment() as the vulnerable logic for zero-byte CONTINUATION frame flood CPU DoS.

unsafe-deserialization-marshallingdecoder/vulnerability_report.json identifies codec-marshalling MarshallingDecoder.java and CompatibleMarshallingDecoder.java readObject() calls as the vulnerable operation.
http2-rapid-reset-mitigated/vulnerability_report.json has status=no_vuln, so it is context only and is not a qualifying advisory match.
